text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY — FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Patient: Margaret Tran
Age: 63
ECOG PS: 1

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Return visit for reassessment following completion of 4 cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed. Discussing maintenance therapy options and clinical trial eligibility.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Tran is a 63-year-old Vietnamese-American woman with stage IV (M1c) lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 21 L858R-mutant, TP53 co-mutant, who presents today for oncology follow-up following completion of 4 cycles of second-line platinum-based chemotherapy. Her disease history is notable for initial presentation approximately 16 months ago with a 4.8 cm right lower lobe mass and ipsilateral mediastinal adenopathy (stations 4R and 7), staged at IIIB (T3N2M0). She was treated with first-line osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily with an excellent initial response — primary mass shrunk to 1.8 cm by 9 months and mediastinal nodes cleared — but developed radiographic and pathologic progression at 14 months. At that time, restaging demonstrated re-enlargement of the right lower lobe primary to 3.1 cm, a new right adrenal metastasis (1.4 cm), new bilateral pulmonary nodules, and recurrent station 4R adenopathy. Brain MRI simultaneously revealed three new intracranial metastases (right cerebellum 1.1 cm, left frontal 0.8 cm, right parietal 0.6 cm). Repeat tissue biopsy of the adrenal metastasis confirmed metastatic lung adenocarcinoma, and NGS from that specimen identified acquired EGFR T790M as the dominant resistance mechanism, with subclonal PIK3CA E545K and low-level CDK4 amplification as potential co-contributors. No high-level MET amplification, no ERBB2 amplification, no bypass track fusions identified.

She subsequently underwent Gamma Knife SRS to all three brain metastases (right cerebellum 20 Gy/1 fx, left frontal 22 Gy/1 fx, right parietal 24 Gy/1 fx), tolerated without acute neurological sequelae. Post-SRS she experienced grade 2 fatigue and a brief grade 1 headache managed with acetaminophen and a dexamethasone taper. Osimertinib was discontinued at the time of confirmed progression, and carboplatin AUC 5 plus pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 every 21 days was initiated as second-line systemic therapy. Cycle 2 was complicated by grade 3 thrombocytopenia (platelet nadir 42K on day 14) and grade 2 anemia (Hgb 8.9 g/dL) requiring carboplatin dose reduction to AUC 4 and a one-week delay for cycle 3; she received a single-donor platelet transfusion for symptomatic epistaxis. No febrile neutropenia. Restaging CT after cycle 3 demonstrated reduction of the right lower lobe mass from 3.1 cm to 2.4 cm, stable adrenal metastasis, stable bilateral pulmonary nodules, and an excellent intracranial response to SRS (cerebellar lesion 0.6 cm with central necrosis, frontal and parietal lesions continuing to regress). She has now completed cycle 4 (carboplatin AUC 4/pemetrexed full dose), and today's visit is focused on assessment for maintenance therapy and clinical trial eligibility.

She reports that fatigue remains her most bothersome complaint — she describes it as a "heavy tiredness" that limits her afternoon activities, roughly grade 1-2. She managed to teach part-time through most of the chemotherapy cycles, though took several sick days around days 10-14 of each cycle when fatigue and nausea peaked. Dyspnea has improved substantially compared to her baseline at progression; she is now walking 1-2 miles without significant breathlessness. Nausea has been manageable with scheduled ondansetron 8 mg, which she continues to use on treatment days and PRN. She notes some residual fingertip paresthesias bilaterally, right more than left, which she attributes to the carboplatin — no burning dysesthesias, no functional impairment. Appetite is fair; she has not lost additional weight since starting chemo and in fact reports a slight recovery. No headaches, no focal neurological symptoms, no visual changes. No new bone pain. No hemoptysis. Bowel function regular.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Fatigue grade 1-2. No fever. No night sweats. Weight stable, slight improvement from nadir.
Pulmonary: Dyspnea markedly improved from progression baseline. Mild exertional limitation. No hemoptysis. Mild residual cough.
Neurological: No headaches, seizures, or focal deficits. Bilateral fingertip paresthesias grade 1 (consistent with carboplatin-related peripheral neuropathy).
Gastrointestinal: Grade 1 nausea, ondansetron-controlled. Appetite fair. No vomiting. Bowel function regular.
Hematologic: No active bleeding. Epistaxis resolved after platelet transfusion last cycle.
Dermatologic: Grade 1 residual acneiform rash on face (EGFR-related from prior Tagrisso, minimally persistent). No new rash.
Musculoskeletal: No arthralgias. No new bone pain.
Endocrine: Hypothyroidism — patient reports feeling her thyroid symptoms are stable.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
(Previously documented; no interim changes.)
- Stage IV (M1c) lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R/TP53 co-mutant (diagnosed originally as stage IIIB, now metastatic at progression)
- Hypertension — well-controlled
- Hypothyroidism — stable on levothyroxine
- Prior SRS to 3 brain metastases (right cerebellum, left frontal, right parietal) — treated, good imaging response

SOCIAL HISTORY
High school biology teacher, teaching part-time during treatment. Vietnamese-American, immigrated at age 14. Former smoker — quit 20 years ago, approximately 12 pack-year history. Lives with husband; two adult children, one locally. Strong family support system. Good health literacy — very engaged in understanding her disease and treatment options.

FAMILY HISTORY
Mother with breast cancer (diagnosed in her 60s). No known lung cancer in first-degree relatives. No known hereditary cancer syndrome. Germline analysis from initial tumor biopsy NGS was negative for pathogenic variants in cancer predisposition genes.

ALLERGIES
No known drug allergies. Tolerated carboplatin without hypersensitivity through 4 cycles (note: cumulative carboplatin exposure — hypersensitivity risk increases with subsequent courses).

MEDICATIONS
- Levothyroxine 88 mcg orally daily
- Amlodipine 5 mg orally daily
- Folic acid 400 mcg orally daily (pemetrexed supplementation — continue)
- Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM every 9 weeks (last given cycle 3 week 3 — due again next week)
- Ondansetron 8 mg orally every 8 hours PRN nausea
- Dexamethasone 4 mg BID day prior, day of, and day after pemetrexed (per pemetrexed premedication protocol)
- Loperamide PRN
- Topical clindamycin 1% gel to face (for residual EGFR rash — now using intermittently)
- Acetaminophen PRN
Osimertinib DISCONTINUED at time of progression.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vitals: BP 126/78 mmHg, HR 74 bpm (regular), RR 14, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 58.2 kg (up from 57.1 kg last visit).

General: Well-appearing woman in no acute distress. Alert, engaged, conversational. Mild fatigue evident but no acute ill appearance.
HEENT: Scattered grade 1 papulopustular lesions over nasal bridge and bilateral cheeks consistent with residual EGFR-related acneiform change, improved from peak. No paronychia visible today. EOMI, PERRL.
Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Decreased breath sounds at right base, mildly diminished compared to left. No wheezing. No frank dullness to percussion suggesting effusion. No crackles.
Cardiac: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No palpable hepatomegaly. No right flank tenderness.
Extremities: No edema. Bilateral fingertip decreased sensation on light touch, worse at tips of index and middle fingers bilaterally — consistent with grade 1 peripheral neuropathy.
Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Gait normal. No focal motor or sensory deficits. No cerebellar signs on finger-nose or tandem gait.
Skin: Scattered grade 1 acneiform lesions face/cheeks as above. No nail changes or paronychia currently.

---

LAB RESULTS

CBC (day 21 of cycle 4, prior to today's visit):
- WBC 5.8 K/uL (recovered)
- Hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL (grade 1 anemia, stable, improved from nadir)
- Platelets 148 K/uL (recovered from prior grade 3 nadir)
- ANC 3,200/uL

CMP:
- Creatinine 0.89 mg/dL, estimated CrCl 66 mL/min (adequate)
- BUN 14 mg/dL
- AST 28 U/L, ALT 22 U/L, Alk Phos 64 U/L (all normal)
- Total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL
- Albumin 3.6 g/dL
- Sodium 138, Potassium 4.0, CO2 25 (all normal)

TSH: 3.4 mIU/L (stable, within normal limits on current levothyroxine dose)

Magnesium: 1.9 mg/dL (low normal — will recheck; relevant for pemetrexed and potential trial eligibility)

---

IMAGING RESULTS

Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (reviewed, obtained after cycle 3): Right lower lobe primary lesion decreased from 3.1 cm (at osimertinib progression) to 2.4 cm. Right adrenal metastasis stable at 1.4 cm. Prior bilateral pulmonary nodules stable. No new lesions identified. No pleural effusion.

MRI brain (concurrent with post-cycle 3 CT): Right cerebellar SRS site decreased to 0.6 cm with central necrosis consistent with treatment response. Left frontal lesion decreased to 0.4 cm. Right parietal lesion decreased to 0.3 cm. No new intracranial lesions. No leptomeningeal enhancement. Excellent response to Gamma Knife.

(Restaging imaging after cycle 4 not yet performed — will be obtained per protocol.)

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV (M1c) lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R/TP53 co-mutant, acquired T790M at osimertinib progression. Currently post-4 cycles carboplatin (AUC 4)/pemetrexed, with partial response. Brain metastases treated with Gamma Knife SRS — excellent intracranial response confirmed on MRI.

   Completed the planned 4-cycle induction phase of carboplatin/pemetrexed. Hematologic toxicity required carboplatin dose reduction in cycle 2 (grade 3 thrombocytopenia) and delayed cycle 3 by one week; cycles 3 and 4 were delivered at AUC 4 with acceptable tolerance. Given partial response and now completed platinum-containing induction, the options for ongoing systemic therapy include: (a) pemetrexed single-agent maintenance, which is standard of care and has established benefit in the setting of non-squamous NSCLC following platinum doublet; or (b) clinical trial enrollment, which the patient and family have very clearly expressed strong interest in.

   Plan: Refer urgently to thoracic oncology clinical trials program. Ms. Tran is a potentially excellent trial candidate — EGFR L858R primary mutation, confirmed acquired T790M, post-third-generation TKI progression, now post-platinum doublet with maintained ECOG PS 1 and adequate organ function. Will confirm whether there are open studies targeting this resistance profile (EGFR/MET bispecifics, EGFR fourth-generation agents, combinations). In the meantime, initiate pemetrexed single-agent maintenance (Alimta 500 mg/m2 IV q21 days) as a bridge, continuing folic acid and B12 supplementation. Continue dexamethasone premedication per protocol.

2. Peripheral neuropathy, grade 1, carboplatin-related. Bilateral fingertip paresthesias, no functional impairment, no worsening noted on exam. No intervention required. Will monitor — if grade 2 or worsening, may impact trial eligibility. Counsel patient regarding early reporting of changes.

3. Anemia, grade 1 (Hgb 9.8 g/dL). Stable, recovered from grade 2 nadir after cycle 2. No transfusion indication at this time. Will continue monitoring. Iron studies pending at next visit.

4. Hypertension — well-controlled on amlodipine 5 mg. BP 126/78 today. No changes.

5. Hypothyroidism — stable, TSH 3.4, in range on levothyroxine 88 mcg. No changes.

6. Intracranial metastases, treated with SRS — patient remains neurologically intact with no clinical symptoms referrable to brain lesions. Excellent radiographic response documented on most recent MRI. Continued surveillance MRI brain per standard schedule (approximately every 3 months).

7. Fatigue (grade 1-2) and residual nausea (grade 1) — continue ondansetron PRN. Discussed energy conservation strategies. Encouraged gradual return to activity as tolerated. Fatigue expected to improve as she moves off platinum-containing therapy.

8. EGFR-related acneiform rash, grade 1 — continue topical clindamycin intermittently. Anticipate possible recurrence if enrolled in trial with EGFR-directed agent. Patient is well-versed in skin care management from prior osimertinib experience.

9. Follow-up: Return in 3 weeks for cycle 1 of pemetrexed maintenance (or trial initiation if enrollment completed sooner). Full restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to be obtained prior to that visit to establish post-cycle 4 baseline and document disease status entering maintenance/trial. MRI brain to follow at ~3-month interval unless new neurological symptoms arise.

Time spent: Approximately 50 minutes, the majority spent in direct patient education, counseling, and clinical decision-making discussion with patient and her husband who accompanied her today. All questions answered.

Electronically signed: Attending Physician, Thoracic Oncology
